section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a diagnostic impression/synthesis by the clinician, not an objective exam or test result, and belongs in the Assessment section.

section_miscategorizations.text: Physical exam reveals tenderness at the medial epicondyle with wrist flexion.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Tenderness on exam is already described as a patient-reported symptom in HPI; here it is explicitly labeled as a physical exam finding and should be in Findings, not HPI. (Note: in the provided note, the same content appears in HPI as patient report and in Findings as exam; the exam phrasing is correctly in Findings, but the HPI version is miscategorized.)

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient also reports pain radiating down their arm with wrist flexion and moderate tenderness at the medial epicondyle with wrist flexion and resisted pronation of the right forearm.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence describes specific provoked tenderness and pain with resisted maneuvers, which are characteristic of physical exam findings rather than typical patient-reported history, and could mislead a reader about what was actually examined.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Fatigue is a separate, chronic issue not clearly linked to the elbow complaint; it would be more clearly organized as an additional active problem in the Assessment, though its presence in HPI is not highly misleading.

This is a review-of-systems-type negative finding; while often documented with HPI in some styles, under this template it aligns better with objective/ROS findings than with the focused elbow pain history.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient presents with symptoms consistent with lateral epicondylitis.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The diagnostic label given (lateral epicondylitis) conflicts with the clearly medial-epicondyle-focused history and exam; while in the correct section type, the specific diagnosis text is clinically inconsistent and could mislead a reader about the problem being assessed.

Although formally a plan item, it is unrelated to the documented chief complaint and HPI (elbow pain) and appears to address a separate, undocumented problem (diabetes), which can mislead about the focus of this encounter under the given template semantics.

section_miscategorizations.text: Order a referral to diabetic counseling.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Similar to the A1C order, this is a plan for diabetes management without corresponding diabetes-related history, findings, or assessment in the appropriate sections, creating a mismatch that can confuse the reader about the visit's assessed problems.

section_miscategorizations.text: Increase metformin dosage to 1000mg twice daily.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a diabetes medication adjustment with no diabetes problem listed in Assessment or described in HPI/Findings; while structurally a plan item, its lack of linkage to an assessed problem makes its placement misleading in the context of this template.
